construct: Analyses spatial patterns, human-environmental interactions and applies geographical tools and techniques to interpret information and promote sustainable use of environmental resources.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies map reading skills to understand spatial relationships in real life

subSkills.label: Interprets maps for geographical relations such as time and angle of elevation to navigate the physical world

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrates understanding of earth's natural processes and their effects

subSkills.label: Understands earth's natural processes and effects; identifies mitigation and adaptation strategies
topicAliases: The Earth in the Solar System
Shape and Size of the Earth
Mitigation and Adaptation to Climate Change

elementsOfConstruct.description: Understands demographic dynamics and its relationship with the environment and humanity in order to identify strategies for sustainable development

subSkills.label: Understands population dynamics and connectivity to nature for sustainable development strategies

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrates understanding of resource utilisation and sustainability

subSkills.label: Understands natural resource use and applies sustainable practices for economic and social benefit

elementsOfConstruct.description: Conducts geographical inquiry using appropriate research methods

subSkills.label: Carries out geographical inquiry and investigates human impact on the environment
unverifiedTopicAliases: Planning and Preparing for Field Work
subSkills.note: SBA-emphasis inquiry EoC; Final Exam covers EoC1-4 (sections A-D), EoC5 mainly SBA fieldwork/projects.

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 5 tasks/100. All 5 EoC. Assignments, field trips/case study, quizzes, discussions, tests.
SBA Form 3: 5 tasks/100. All 5 EoC. Combined SBA 200 marks (30%).
4 sections A-D (EoC1-4), 2 items each (8 total); candidates choose 1 per section (4 answered). Duration 2h. 70%. EoC5 SBA-focused.

rules: SBA: teacher-developed scoring tools for assignments, fieldwork, quizzes, presentations and tests.
Final Exam: ECZ scoring; each chosen item based on its section EoC scenario/stimulus.
Map-reading items require accurate use of scale, symbols, direction and measurement.
Fieldwork/report tasks scored on research procedure and report structure.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Map reading literacy and spatial relationships in a real-life navigation/land-use scenario
